channel.title: Physics on RadixArk Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Physics on RadixArk Blog

item.title: Physics Simulations in the Cloud: Solving Naviar-Stokes
item.description:
When simulating fluid dynamics, engineers rely on the Navier-Stokes equations to model the turbulent flow of air over a wing or water through a pipe.

The problem? Calculating these over millions of grid points in 3D space is incredibly computationally intensive.

The Incompressible Flow Equations

The momentum equation for an incompressible fluid with constant viscosity is written as:

$$ \rho \left( \frac{\partial \mathbf{u}}{\partial t} + \mathbf{u} \cdot \nabla \mathbf{u} \right) = -\nabla p + \mu \nabla^2 \mathbf{u} + \mathbf{f} $$

Where:
